step_by_step_reasoning: The architecture shows a regulated enterprise landing zone managed via AWS Organizations. We identify the core governance account at the top, managing separate accounts for workload (EC2), SSO Bifrost (Fargate and IAM), Compliance (AWS Config and Inspector), and Auditing (CloudTrail). Visual arrows indicate AWS Organizations orchestrating the accounts, and a SAML-based authentication flow from SSO Bifrost (Fargate) to the Workload (EC2) instances.

graph.name: Volkswagen Financial Services: Scalable and Compliant Landing Zone
graph.notes: A multi-account landing zone structure managing 1400 accounts with centralized auditing, compliance, and SSO governance using AWS Organizations.

nodes.name: AWS Organizations
nodes.notes: Centrally manages and automates baselines for 1400+ AWS accounts.

nodes.name: SSO Bifrost (Fargate)
nodes.notes: Hosts Red Hat SSO (Bifrost) to provide federated SAML access to workloads.

nodes.name: AWS IAM
nodes.notes: Manages user permissions and identity governance centrally.

nodes.name: Workload EC2
nodes.notes: Represents the active business workloads isolated in staging accounts.

nodes.name: AWS Config
nodes.notes: Tracks resource configurations across accounts to generate compliance reports.

nodes.name: Amazon Inspector
nodes.notes: Performs vulnerability scanning on EC2 instances and containers.

nodes.name: AWS CloudTrail
nodes.notes: Aggregates API logs centrally in the dedicated auditing account.

edges.notes: Authenticates users to workloads via SAML.
